items.title: Dow closes at 52,925.15 after hitting new all-time intraday high; Nasdaq mixed
items.content: The Dow Jones Industrial Average closed at 52,925.15 on July 7, after earlier hitting a new all-time intraday high. S&P 500 ended virtually flat at 7,483.24, while Nasdaq dropped 0.8% as chip stocks suffered.

items.title: Dow surpasses 53,000 for first time; sets new closing record after holiday weekend
items.content: The Dow Jones Industrial Average closed above 53,000 for the first time on July 6, marking a historic milestone. S&P 500 gained 1.77%, Nasdaq up 2.12% in the broad rally.

items.title: Hang Seng Index surged 3.0% to close at 24,199
items.content: The Hang Seng Index surged 3.0%, or 703 points, to close at 24,199 on July 8. Notable gainers included SMIC (+4.2%), Xiaomi (+1.7%), and Lenovo (+3.9%).

items.title: Hong Kong stocks rebound as Alibaba, SMIC, tech giants lift market sentiment
items.content: Hong Kong stocks rallied as concerns eased over IPO lock-up expiries. Hang Seng Tech Index heavily outperformed the broader market in the July 8 rally.

items.title: May PCE inflation at 4.07% year-over-year, well above Fed's 2% target
items.content: The May Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) report showed headline PCE inflation at 4.07% YoY, with core PCE at 3.4%. Three-month annualized pace hit 6.28%.

items.title: FOMC Minutes June 16-17: Staff estimated total PCE at 4.1% in May
items.content: FOMC minutes released July 9 showed staff estimated total PCE price inflation rose to 4.1% in May, boosted by consumer energy prices linked to Iran conflict. Core PCE estimated at 3.4%.

items.title: Measurement tweaks will make inflation data look better; Core PCE at 3.4%
items.content: Core PCE inflation was 3.4% for the 12 months ended in May, above Fed's 2% target every month since March 2021. New measurement methodology could help data look more favorable.

items.title: US and Iran threaten return to war after fiercest exchange of fire since truce
items.content: Both sides threaten return to hostilities as ceasefire frays. Iran disputes US-sponsored southern route through Strait of Hormuz, claiming breach of ceasefire MoU. Trump said at NATO summit he thought ceasefire was over.

items.title: 2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis: Iran closed strait again in June; US Navy widened route
items.content: On June 19 Trump announced renewed ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah, but Iran closed the strait again on June 20, citing Israeli actions. On June 27, US Navy announced widened route through the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: China, Hong Kong stocks gain as chip, AI rally lead rebound
items.content: CSI Semiconductor Index rallied 4.1% and CSI AI Index jumped 3.9%, leading a broad rebound in Chinese equities as tech sentiment improved.

items.title: Zhipu AI and Iluvatar CoreX Semiconductor shares surge on HK$38.5B fundraising plans
items.content: Zhipu AI (Knowledge Atlas) shares climbed as much as 9% to HK$1,989 after announcing secondary placement plans totaling HK$38.5 billion alongside Iluvatar CoreX Semiconductor.

items.title: Macquarie says now is the 'best time' to buy Chinese AI chip stocks
items.content: Macquarie analysts recommend buying Chinese AI chip stocks, citing government support for domestic AI chip firms and US export controls on Nvidia GPUs. Huawei Ascend chips seen as key beneficiary.
